title: Can't start hyperv machine if I have a wsl machine running
body:
### Issue Description

Hello,

I am encountering an error when I try to start a hyperv machine when I have wsl machine running:
```
> podman machine ls --all-providers                
NAME                   VM TYPE     CREATED        LAST UP            CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE  
podman-machine-wsl     wsl         6 minutes ago  Currently running  2           2GiB        100GiB         
podman-machine-hyperv  hyperv      8 minutes ago  4 minutes ago      2           3.725GiB    52GiB   
> podman machine start podman-machine-hyperv                                                                                                                                       
Starting machine "podman-machine-hyperv"                                                                                                                                                               
Error: unable to start: podman-machine-wsl already starting or running on the wsl provider: only one VM can be active at a time   
```
However I have no trouble if I start the hyperv machine first, and after that start the wsl machine:
```
> podman machine ls --all-providers      
NAME                   VM TYPE     CREATED         LAST UP             CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE 
podman-machine-hyperv  hyperv      23 minutes ago  Currently running   2           3.725GiB    52GiB    
podman-machine-wsl     wsl         21 minutes ago  About a minute ago  2           2GiB        100GiB
> podman machine start podman-machine-wsl      
Starting machine "podman-machine-wsl"      
API forwarding listening on: npipe:////./pipe/podman-machine-wsl                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         Another process was listening on the default Docker API pipe address.  
You can still connect Docker API clients by setting DOCKER HOST using the                                                                                                                              
following powershell command in your terminal session:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
$Env:DOCKER_HOST = 'npipe:////./pipe/podman-machine-wsl'                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              
Or in a classic CMD prompt:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           
set DOCKER_HOST=npipe:////./pipe/podman-machine-wsl                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   
Alternatively, terminate the other process and restart podman machine.                                                                                                                                 
Machine "podman-machine-wsl" started successfully
> podman machine ls --all-providers                                                                                                                                                
NAME                   VM TYPE     CREATED         LAST UP            CPUS        MEMORY      DISK SIZE                                                                                                
podman-machine-hyperv  hyperv      26 minutes ago  Currently running  2           3.725GiB    52GiB                                                                                                    
podman-machine-wsl     wsl         23 minutes ago  Currently running  2           2GiB        100GiB  
```

I know that it's probably a extremely edge case, but I wanted to know if this behavior is expected.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. Start from no podman machines
2. Create and start a podman wsl machine
3. Create and start a podman hyperv machine


### Describe the results you received

```
Error: unable to start: podman-machine-wsl already starting or running on the wsl provider: only one VM can be active at a time   
```

### Describe the results you expected

```
Machine "podman-machine-hyperv" started successfully
```

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Privileged

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

_No response_

### Additional information

_No response_

comments.body: I believe this is deliberate; the `podman machine` set of commands are only intended to manage a single active VM at the same time.

I think this is expected. Here is a related PR for macOS: https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/25139. I’m not sure about the Windows environment, but I suspect the reason is the same. I think @baude can answer this.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
So is it a bug or feature? On windows it is possible to run more than one machine (of the same provider) at the same time. Is it also a case for different providers?
